name: TAO Retest Hold De-Risk v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear fast enough. The edge should come from entering only after retest acceptance, then cutting exposure quickly when the expected second leg does not show up.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest holds are too rare, if the retest qualifier conflicts with direction confirmation, or if continuation is too weak to justify even the faster de-risking profile.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A breakout does not extend immediately, retests the hold zone, then continues only if acceptance is real. If the second leg does not appear quickly, the position should be reduced early.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transitional markets often trap immediate breakout chasing but reward delayed retest entries. Time-based de-risk protects against the common failure mode where expansion never arrives.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is useful here because its structurally different volatility should stress-test the retest thesis while still fitting the allowed basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If TAO behaves as pure noise rather than structured retests, the continuation leg will not develop before the time-based de-risk triggers.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but the second leg arrives too slowly, extend the early de-risk window slightly before tightening the retest rules.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest hold and DI confirmation support each other
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept reasonable by broad retest-distance rules

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14 by cutting 50% size
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14 by cutting 50% size
